articles.title: Brookline voters overwhelmingly approve tax override to avoid school, public safety cuts
articles.description:
Nearly 60 percent of voters approved Brookline’s Question 1, with 8,859 votes for yes and 5,826 voting no, according to official results shared with Boston.com. 

The post Brookline voters overwhelmingly approve tax override to avoid school, public safety cuts appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Brookline voters approved a tax override to prevent cuts to schools and public safety.
Nearly 60% of voters supported Question 1, totaling 8,859 yes votes.
The approval ensures continued funding for essential services in Brookline.

articles.title: Do the Bruins have a potential No. 1 center already on their roster?
articles.description:
"There's not even 32 of them in the league."

The post Do the Bruins have a potential No. 1 center already on their roster? appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Bruins may have a potential No. 1 center in their current roster.
James Hagens and Fraser Minten are highlighted as promising players.
Local fans could see significant roster changes in the upcoming season.

articles.title: Maine police arrest victim’s boyfriend for 1987 murder
articles.description:
Maine police arrested the victim's former boyfriend, as the Maine Attorney General seek to charge him with murder, police said.

The post Maine police arrest victim’s boyfriend for 1987 murder appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Maine police arrested a former boyfriend in connection with a 1987 murder case.
The Maine Attorney General plans to charge him with murder soon.
This case may resonate with Boston residents concerned about cold cases and justice.

articles.title: Is Patriots’ 2026 schedule actually easier than many expected? This win-projection metric says so.
articles.description:
Sharp Football Analysis has the Patriots with the 12th-easiest schedule in the NFL this season.

The post Is Patriots’ 2026 schedule actually easier than many expected? This win-projection metric says so. appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Sharp Football Analysis ranks the Patriots' 2026 schedule as the 12th-easiest in the NFL.
Easier schedule may boost local fan optimism for the team's performance in 2026.
Improved win projections could enhance game attendance and local business revenue in Boston.
